---
title: "UPC Surveys"
subtitle: "By Gina Contolini. Gina.Contolini@noaa.gov"
author: "Greater Farallones Association and Greater Farallones and Cordell Bank National Marine Sanctuaries"
date: "Updated on `r Sys.Date()`"
output: 
  pdf_document
editor_options: 
  chunk_output_type: console
---
```{r setup, include = FALSE, echo = FALSE, message = FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
	echo = FALSE,
	message = FALSE,
	warning = FALSE,
	fig.keep = 'all'
)

# the shortcut for pipe is ctrl+shift+M
#### Install and load packages ####
library(car) #companion to applied regression
library(tidyverse) # all tidyverse packages including tidyr, dplyr, and ggplot2
library(ggpubr) # plot aesthetics
library(flextable) # tidy display table package
library(googlesheets4)

#### Create plot theme ####
theme_set(theme( 
   panel.background = element_blank(), # remove panel background
   # panel.grid.minor.x = element_blank(), # remove minor x axis grid line
   # panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(), # remove major x axis grid line
   panel.grid = element_blank(), # remove all grid lines
   axis.text = element_text(size = 13,color = 'black'), #makes axis labels bigger
   axis.title = element_text(size = 15), #makes axis title bigger and bold
   # axis.ticks = element_blank(),
   # axis.title.y = element_text(margin=margin(0,10,0,5)), #pushes the y axis title away from the y axis labels
   # axis.text.x = element_text(margin=margin(0,0,20,0)), #pushes the x axis title away from the x axis labels and pushes the bars down closer to the x axis labels
   legend.title = element_text(size = 16),
   legend.text  = element_text(size = 16),
   legend.background = element_blank(),
   panel.border = element_rect(colour = "black", fill = NA, linewidth = 0.5), # border around the whole plot
   plot.title = element_text(size = 15)
   #legend.key = element_blank()
))
```

```{r read in data}
upc <- read_csv('GFNMS UPC data entry (all years) - load_to_R.csv') %>% 
  dplyr::mutate(site = replace(site, site == 'timber', 'Timber')) %>% 
  dplyr::mutate(site = replace(site, site == 'fort_ross', 'Fort Ross')) %>%   
  dplyr::mutate(site = replace(site, site == 'shell_beach', 'Shell Beach')) %>% 
  dplyr::mutate(site = replace(site, site == 'pebble_beach', 'Pebble Beach')) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(site = replace(site, site == 'ocean', 'Ocean')) %>% 
  dplyr::mutate(site = replace(site, site == 'gerstle', 'Gerstle'))
# make year factor for plotting
upc$year = as.factor(upc$year)
```

```{r check data for completeness, include = FALSE}
# assign transect ID for ease of sorting later
upc$transect_ID <- upc %>% 
  dplyr::group_by(year, site, subsite, region, zone, observer, transect) %>%
  group_indices()

# number of surveys completed per site per year
tot_upc_site_year <- upc %>% 
  dplyr::count(year, site, transect_ID) %>% 
  dplyr::count(year, site) %>% 
  mutate(survey_type = 'UPC') %>% 
  rename(N_yr = n)

# # compared with swath inventory. This is from the subtidal_swath.Rmd scrpt.
# full_join(tot_survey_site_year, tot_upc_site_year) %>% 
#   arrange(year, site) %>% 
#   flextable()

# check that there are 30 observations per transect.
upc %>% 
  dplyr::filter(category == 'SUBSTRATE') %>%
  dplyr::group_by(transect_ID) %>% # group by replicate transect
  dplyr::summarise(count = sum(count)) %>% 
  dplyr::filter(!count == 30) # there are only three missing 1 to 10 data points.
# Transect 7 was never completed because they didn't survey the last 10m segment
# Transect 10 same story: didn't survey last 10 m, but sometimes observed 11 points instead of 10
# Transect 17: forgot one point

```

```{r inventory of surveys}
tot_upc_site_year %>% flextable()
```

```{r substrate}
# how much of each transect was bedrock, boulder, cobble, etc?
upc_substrate <- upc %>%
  dplyr::group_by(year, month, day, site, subsite, region, zone, transect, transect_ID, category, classcode) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(count = sum(count)) %>% # sum the three segments per transect
  dplyr::filter(category == 'SUBSTRATE') %>%
  dplyr::group_by(year, month, day, site, subsite, zone, transect, transect_ID, classcode) %>% # group by replicate transect
  dplyr::summarise(count = sum(count)) %>% 
  dplyr::mutate(proportion = count / sum(count)) 

# Check that all proportions add to one.
# upc_substrate %>%
#   group_by(transect_ID) %>%
#   summarise(prop_sum = sum(proportion)) %>%
#   filter(!prop_sum == 1) # zero rows do not equal one.

# # Proportion of substrates at all sites, not separated by year
# upc_substrate %>% 
#   # dplyr::group_by(site, classcode) %>% 
#   # dplyr::summarise(mean_prop = mean(proportion), n = n()) %>% 
#   ggplot(aes(x = classcode, y = proportion)) +
#   facet_wrap(~site) +
#   geom_boxplot() +
#   ggtitle('Proportion of substrates in UPC surveys all years') +
#   scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0,1)) +
#   theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 55, vjust = 1, hjust = 1))

# Stacked column chart
upc_substrate %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x = site, y = count, fill = classcode)) +
  geom_col() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 55, vjust = 1, hjust = 1))

# check for changes at FRC and TC
upc_substrate %>% 
  filter(site %in% c('Fort Ross', 'Timber', 'Gerstle', 'Ocean')) %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x = year, y = count, fill = classcode)) +
  facet_wrap(~site) +
  geom_col() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 55, vjust = 1, hjust = 1))
  
# # boxplots
# upc_substrate %>% 
#   filter(site %in% c('Fort Ross', 'Timber')) %>% 
#   ggplot(aes(x = classcode, y = proportion)) +
#   # facet_wrap(~site) +
#   facet_grid(site~year) +
#   scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0,1)) +
#   geom_boxplot() +
#   ggtitle('Proportion of substrates in UPC surveys') +
#   theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 55, vjust = 1, hjust = 1))
```

```{r rugosity}
# Rugosity proportions

# add up the counts in each transect
upc_rugosity <- upc %>%
  dplyr::filter(category == 'RELIEF') %>% 
  dplyr::group_by(year, month, day, site, subsite, region, zone, transect, transect_ID, category, classcode) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(count = sum(count)) %>% # sum the three segments per transect
  dplyr::mutate(classcode = factor(classcode, levels = c('0-10cm',  '10cm-1m', '1m-2m', '>2m'))) %>% 
  dplyr::group_by(year, month, day, site, subsite, zone, transect, transect_ID, classcode) %>% # group by replicate transect
  dplyr::summarise(count = sum(count)) %>% 
  dplyr::mutate(proportion = count / sum(count)) 

# # Check that all proportions add to one.
# upc_rugosity %>%
#   group_by(transect_ID) %>%
#   summarise(prop_sum = sum(proportion)) %>%
#   filter(!prop_sum == 1) # zero rows do not equal one.

# Proportion at all sites, not separated by year

upc_rugosity %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x = site, y = count, fill = classcode)) +
  geom_col() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 55, vjust = 1, hjust = 1))

# ## boxplots
# upc_rugosity %>% 
#   # dplyr::group_by(site, classcode) %>% 
#   # dplyr::summarise(mean_prop = mean(proportion), n = n()) %>% 
#   ggplot(aes(x = classcode, y = proportion)) +
#   facet_wrap(~site) +
#   geom_boxplot() +
#   ggtitle('Proportion of rugosity in UPC surveys all years') +
#   theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 55, vjust = 1, hjust = 1))

# # This is how to make stacked bars that add to one
# upc_rugosity %>%
#   dplyr::group_by(site, classcode) %>%
#   dplyr::summarise(count = sum(count)) %>%
#   dplyr::mutate(proportion = count / sum(count)) %>%
#   ggplot(aes(x = site, y = proportion, fill = classcode)) +
#   geom_col() +
#   scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0,0), limits = c(0,1.02))+
#   ggtitle('Total relief proportion in UPC surveys')

# check for changes at FRC and TC
upc_rugosity %>% 
  filter(site %in% c('Fort Ross', 'Timber', 'Gerstle', 'Ocean')) %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x = year, y = count, fill = classcode)) +
  facet_wrap(~site) +
  geom_col() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 55, vjust = 1, hjust = 1))
  
# #box plots
# upc_rugosity %>% 
#   filter(site %in% c('Fort Ross', 'Timber')) %>% 
#   ggplot(aes(x = classcode, y = proportion)) +
#   facet_grid(site ~ year) +
#   scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0,1)) +
#   geom_boxplot() +
#   theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, vjust = 1, hjust = 1)) +
#   ggtitle('Proportion of rugosity in UPC surveys')
```

```{r cover}
# Rugosity proportions

# add up the counts in each transect
upc_cover <- upc %>%
  dplyr::filter(category == 'COVER') %>% 
  dplyr::group_by(year, month, day, site, subsite, region, zone, transect, transect_ID, category, classcode) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(count = sum(count)) %>% # sum the three segments per transect
  dplyr::group_by(year, month, day, site, subsite, zone, transect, transect_ID, classcode) %>% # group by replicate transect
  dplyr::summarise(count = sum(count)) %>% 
  dplyr::mutate(proportion = count / sum(count)) 

# # Check that all proportions add to one.
# upc_cover %>% 
#   group_by(transect_ID) %>% 
#   summarise(prop_sum = sum(proportion)) %>% 
#   filter(!prop_sum == 1) # zero rows do not equal one. 

# Proportion of cover at Fort Ross
upc_cover %>% 
  dplyr::filter(site == 'Fort Ross') %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x = classcode, y = proportion)) +
  facet_grid(year~site) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  ggtitle('Proportion of cover in UPC surveys at Fort Ross Cove') +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 55, vjust = 1, hjust = 1))

# Proportion of cover at Timber
upc_cover %>% 
  dplyr::filter(site == 'Timber') %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x = classcode, y = proportion)) +
  facet_grid(year~site) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  ggtitle('Proportion of cover in UPC surveys at Timber Cove') +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 55, vjust = 1, hjust = 1))

# Proportion of cover at Gerstle
upc_cover %>% 
  dplyr::filter(site == 'Gerstle') %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x = classcode, y = proportion)) +
  facet_grid(year~site) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  ggtitle('Proportion of cover in UPC surveys at Gerstle Cove') +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 55, vjust = 1, hjust = 1))

# Proportion of cover at Ocean Cove
upc_cover %>% 
  dplyr::filter(site == 'Ocean') %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x = classcode, y = proportion)) +
  facet_grid(year~site) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  ggtitle('Proportion of cover in UPC surveys at Ocean Cove') +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 55, vjust = 1, hjust = 1))

# Proportion of cover at Shell Beach
upc_cover %>% 
  dplyr::filter(site == 'Shell Beach') %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x = classcode, y = proportion)) +
  facet_grid(year~site) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  ggtitle('Proportion of cover in UPC surveys at Shell Beach') +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 55, vjust = 1, hjust = 1))

# # This is how to make stacked bars that add to one
# upc_rugosity %>%
#   dplyr::group_by(site, classcode) %>%
#   dplyr::summarise(count = sum(count)) %>%
#   dplyr::mutate(proportion = count / sum(count)) %>%
#   ggplot(aes(x = site, y = proportion, fill = classcode)) +
#   geom_col() +
#   scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0,0), limits = c(0,1.02))+
#   ggtitle('Total relief proportion in UPC surveys')


## old code below
# # how much of each transect was each cover category?
# upc  %>%
#   filter(category == 'COVER') %>%
#   dplyr::group_by(site, classcode) %>%
#   dplyr::summarise(count = sum(count)) %>%
#   dplyr::mutate(proportion = count / sum(count)) %>%
#   ggplot(aes(x = site, y = proportion, fill = classcode)) +
#   geom_col() +
#   scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0,0), limits = c(0,1.02))+
#   ggtitle('Total cover proportion in UPC surveys')
```
